--- a/src/agent_loom/memory/README.md
+++ b/src/agent_loom/memory/README.md
@@ -155,6 +155,11 @@ Common flags:
 - `--interactive` open editor for body
 - `--allow-missing-scopes`
 
+Agentic UX notes:
+
+- plural aliases like `--tags`, `--aliases`, `--scopes`, `--links`, `--relateds` are normalized.
+- add/edit responses include hydration feedback (`hydration_summary`) and suggested follow-up commands (`next_actions`).
+
 Examples:
 
 ```
@@ -204,6 +209,34 @@ loom memory edit retry-behavior --scope file:src/worker.py
 loom memory edit retry-behavior --visibility personal
 ```
 
+Agentic UX notes:
+
+- `loom memory edit <id> <text>` is normalized to append mode.
+- if stdin is piped, choose an explicit body mode (`--from-stdin` or `--append-from-stdin`).
+
+### append
+
+Append body updates to an existing note using append-first command ergonomics.
+
+Aliases:
+
+- `loom memory add-note` -> `loom memory append`
+- `loom memory append-note` -> `loom memory append`
+
+Flags:
+
+- `--append` (or `--text` or `--body`) append text
+- `--from-stdin` append from stdin
+- `--related` append `Related: [[...]]` line(s)
+
+Examples:
+
+```
+loom memory append retry-behavior --append "Observed retry spike in production"
+echo "new trace" | loom memory append retry-behavior --from-stdin
+loom memory add-note retry-behavior "More findings"
+```
+
 ### recall
 
 Recall notes with FTS + filters. Default output is JSON.

args.plan:
## Scope
Improve `loom memory` add/edit/append UX so hallucinated-but-plausible inputs still do the expected thing, while making hydration/scaffold behavior more actionable for agents.

## 0) Protocol First (at implementation start)
- Create a new Loom ticket for this work (linked to existing memory UX epic if useful), then log progress notes as each phase lands.
- Recall relevant memory context and keep adding scoped notes for reusable patterns discovered during implementation.

## 1) Harden Agentic Arg Normalization (CLI)
Target: `src/agent_loom/memory/cli.py`

- Extend `_normalize_argv` with a curated alias set for high-frequency hallucinations:
  - `--tags -> --tag`, `--aliases -> --alias`, `--scopes -> --scope`, `--links -> --link`, `--relateds -> --related`.
  - Command-form shorthands for append flows (e.g., `append-note`, `add-note` as normalized append intent).
- Add safe positional fallback for edit/update:
  - `loom memory edit <id> "text"` -> treat as append when no body/append/interactive mode is already set.
- Add stdin-safety behavior for edit/update:
  - if stdin is piped but no explicit consume mode is chosen (`--from-stdin`, `--append-from-stdin`, `--body -`, `--append -`), return structured ARG guidance (instead of silently ignoring input).

Why: minimize failure from plausible AI command generation and prevent silent no-ops.

## 2) First-Class Append Command UX
Targets: `src/agent_loom/memory/cli.py`, `src/agent_loom/memory/core.py`, `src/agent_loom/memory/README.md`

- Add dedicated append-oriented command surface (internally using current edit plumbing):
  - `loom memory append <id> ...` with aliases like `add-note` / `append-note`.
- Keep existing `edit --append` behavior unchanged and compatible.
- Ensure append command supports the most useful agent paths:
  - direct text append
  - strict stdin append
  - related/wikilink append conveniences

+### append
+
+Append body updates to an existing note using append-first command ergonomics.
+
+Aliases:
+
+- `loom memory add-note` -> `loom memory append`
+- `loom memory append-note` -> `loom memory append`
+
+Flags:
+
+- `--append` (or `--text` or `--body`) append text
+- `--from-stdin` append from stdin
+- `--related` append `Related: [[...]]` line(s)
+
+Examples:
+

skill_candidates.body:
---
name: loom-memory-wikilink-hydration
description: Procedure for implementing or changing Loom memory wikilink hydration with deterministic behavior, UX-visible guarantees, and aligned docs/tests.
---

# Loom memory wikilink hydration

Use this when changing wikilink handling in Loom memory (creation, hydration, stubs, or CLI output).

## 1) Define the contract first

- Specify exact user-visible behavior for `[[wikilinks]]`:
  - normalization rules,
  - when linked notes are created,
  - what output the CLI prints.
- Keep behavior deterministic; avoid order-dependent output.

## 2) Implement across the full memory path

Touch all relevant layers together so behavior stays coherent:

- `src/agent_loom/memory/cli.py`
- `src/agent_loom/memory/core.py`
- `src/agent_loom/memory/hydrate.py`
- `src/agent_loom/memory/models.py`

Do not leave partial logic in one layer only.

## 3) Prove UX behavior and hydration logic separately

- Add/update CLI UX assertions in `tests/test_memory_cli_ux.py`:
  - exact stdout/stderr expectations,
  - exit-code expectations.
- Add/update focused hydration tests in `tests/test_memory_link_hydration.py`:
  - deterministic link extraction/hydration,
  - edge cases around link formatting and stub creation.

## 4) Keep docs in lockstep

- Update `src/agent_loom/memory/README.md` in the same change with examples that match real CLI behavior.
- Ensure docs describe behavior users can observe, not internal implementation details.

## 5) Quality gates

Run:

- `uv run ruff check .`
- `uv run basedpyright`
- `uv run pytest tests/test_memory_cli_ux.py tests/test_memory_link_hydration.py`

If these pass, run broader tests as needed.

## Done checklist

- Contract is deterministic and explicit.
- CLI/core/hydrate/models stay aligned.
- UX tests and hydration tests both cover the change.
- README examples match actual behavior.
